zh.lifeSummary: 當John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted於大約1508年出生在英國艾可塔可泰德時，他的父親Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted50歲，母親Agnes Pelesholle36歲。他於1531年8月7日與Margaret Leigh of Walton在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年6月22日在他的家鄉享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted于大约1508年出生在英国艾可塔可泰德时，他的父亲Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted50岁，母亲Agnes Pelesholle36岁。他于1531年8月7日与Margaret Leigh of Walton在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年6月22日在他的家乡享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰希迪 本 倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Alleyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Thaxted
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk and Cambridgeshire): occupational name from Middle English thakester(e), thaxter(e) ‘thatcher’ (Old English thæcestre). The word originally denoted a female thatcher, but came to be used by thatchers of both sexes. Compare Thacker .

variants.name: Thacker
meanings: English (northern): occupational name for a thatcher, from an agent derivative of northern Middle English thack ‘thatch’ (Old Norse thak). Compare Thatcher .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted was born about 1508, in Thaxted, Essex, England, his father, Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted, was 50 and his mother, Agnes Pelesholle, was 36. He married Margaret Leigh of Walton on 7 August 1531, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 22 June 1558, in his hometown, at the age of 51, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted est né environ 1508, à Thaxted, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Agnes Pelesholle, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Leigh of Walton le 7 août 1531, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 22 juin 1558, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat am 7. August 1531 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Margaret Leigh of Walton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 22. Lui sposato Margaret Leigh of Walton il 7 agosto 1531, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 22 giugno 1558, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a City of London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxtedが約1508年にイギリスエセックスサクステドで生まれた時，父Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxtedは50歳，母Agnes Pelesholleは36歳であった。1531年8月7日にイギリスイギリスエセックスでMargaret Leigh of Waltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1558年6月22日に故郷で51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシトイ オフ ロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted(이)가 대략 1508년 영국 에세스 스액테드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Pelesholle(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1531년 8월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Margaret Leigh of Walton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 6월 22일 그의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted nasceu aproximadamente 1508, em Thaxted, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Pelesholle, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Leigh of Walton em 7 de agosto de 1531, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de junho de 1558, em sua cidade natal, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em City of London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Allen or Alleyne of Thaxted родился около 1508 г. в местности такстед, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Alleyne Gent. of Thaxted было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Pelesholle, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Leigh of Walton 7 августа 1531 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 22 июня 1558 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Margaret Leigh of Walton el 7 de agosto de 1531, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 22 de junio de 1558, en su pueblo, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en City of London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
